title: PM Lane 413 Revision A - Project Miner Temp Power Lane 412 Live Admission Roadmap Amendment No-Live Packet

phase_0_discovery.original_roadmap: Historical Lane 413 remains the canonical nine-lane roadmap from PM Lane 414 through PM Lane 422.
phase_0_discovery.render_topology_precedent: APEX Ops Visual System Map still documents Render as the governed mutation-seam and API boundary.
phase_0_discovery.auth_precedent: apps/mutation-seam/app/auth/jwt.py still provides the bearer-token actor dependency pattern.
phase_0_discovery.route_role_precedent: The route-owned role precedent now truthfully resolves through apps/mutation-seam/app/routers/project_import_approvals.py plus apps/mutation-seam/app/project_import_approval_persistence.py rather than a flat app/project_import_approvals.py path.
phase_0_discovery.route_pair_gap: No project-import-contract-support write or readback route exists in apps/mutation-seam/app/** today.
phase_0_discovery.lane_411_revision_c: Lane 411 Revision C records PM+Operations as the corrected role contract for the financial-table family.

inherits_unchanged: purpose and selected outcome of the historical Lane 413 planning packet
failure-mode contract and sha256 idempotency-key construction
multi-scope fixture requirement with PM Lane 416 as the latest mandatory gate
cross-lane gate inheritance for later Lane 280 admission
amended_roadmap: PM Lane 414 - Local Mocked Dry-Run Packet
PM Lane 415 - Dry-Run Envelope Export Packet
PM Lane 416 - Dry-Run Readiness Checkpoint Packet
PM Lane 417 - Dry-Run Readiness Export Packet
PM Lane 418 - Review Bundle Export Packet

new_lane_419.summary: Implement the real Lane 412 route pair in the seam app before any hosted smoke or live-write packet is discussable.
evidence_required: Both routes implemented in apps/mutation-seam/app/** and registered with the seam app router
Bearer-token auth wired through the jwt.py actor dependency pattern
Route-owned PM+Operations role enforcement following the project_import_approvals router plus persistence precedent
Business logic mirrors Lane 414 mock digest, envelope validation, and Lane 415 response-shape contract
dry_run and force_failure are env-gated behind LANE_412_DRY_RUN_ENABLED or a truthfully documented equivalent
When the flag is absent, dry_run and force_failure are ignored
Focused unit tests run against Lane 415 frozen envelope fixtures
Unit tests prove no-write response-shape parity with Lane 414 and Lane 415 artifacts
new_lane_419.gate_to_next_step: The route pair exists in the deployable seam surface, is route-enforced with the PM+Operations contract, supports env-gated dry-run behavior, and passes fixture-backed parity tests without live writes.
blocks_promotion: Routes not registered with the seam app router
Auth not wired through the jwt.py actor dependency pattern
Role enforcement deferred only to DB-layer grants
Role contract still uses Finance instead of Operations
dry_run and force_failure are not env-gated
Response envelopes diverge from Lane 415 frozen exports
Routes attempt live Supabase writes before PM Lane 422

cross_lane_gate_note: Generic Lane 280 and Lane 412 references remain unchanged because they describe the bidirectional admission gate, not the implementation-lane numbering.
validation_steps: Phase 0 confirms historical Lane 413 remains the canonical nine-lane roadmap.
Phase 0 confirms the Render, jwt.py, and router-plus-persistence precedents remain accurate.
Phase 0 confirms Lane 411 Revision C records PM+Operations.
Roadmap enumeration expands to ten lanes, PM Lane 414 through PM Lane 423.
New PM Lane 419 has explicit evidence, gate, and eight blocks-promotion items.
Renumbered PM Lanes 420 through 423 preserve original criteria with lane-number updates only.
First-live-write references shift from PM Lane 421 to PM Lane 422.
Generic Lane 280 and Lane 412 references remain unchanged.
Multi-scope gate at PM Lane 416 remains unchanged.
Failure-mode contract and Option B sequencing remain unchanged.
